activities_projects.title: Famine vs. Food Insecurity Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the progression from chronic food insecurity to a famine state
steps: Contrast the 1943 Bengal Famine with modern localized food shortages
Analyze the 'Starvation' process: explain how declining food production leads to hoarding and hyper-inflation
Identify the 'Vulnerable Groups' who shift from food-insecure to starvation-prone during disasters
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the socio-economic triggers of famine
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical depth in explaining the hunger-famine transition (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping Regional Food Insecurity
activities_projects.objective: Identify geographical and social pockets of hunger in India
steps: Shade regions with high food insecurity (e.g., Kalahandi in Odisha, Palamau in Jharkhand)
Identify the social groups most affected: SCs, STs, and OBCs with low land productivity
Explain the 'Seasonal Hunger' cycle in the context of agricultural laborers versus urban casual workers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the concentration of food insecurity in specific demographics
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the map and socio-economic reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The FCI Logistics Chain
activities_projects.objective: Understand the mechanism of Buffer Stock and the Food Corporation of India
steps: Create a flowchart of the grain journey: Farmer -> MSP -> FCI -> Buffer Stock -> Issue Price -> PDS
Explain the 'Minimum Support Price' (MSP) as a tool for ensuring 'Availability'
Describe how the 'Issue Price' ensures 'Affordability' for the BPL population
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the operational cycle of India's food management system
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and accuracy of the logistics flowchart (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Public Distribution System (PDS) Audit
activities_projects.objective: Critically examine the evolution and effectiveness of rationing
steps: Compare the 'Universal PDS' (pre-1992) with the 'Targeted PDS' (TPDS) introduced in 1997
Discuss the impact of the 'Antyodaya Anna Yojana' (AAY) on the poorest of the poor
Identify two problems mentioned in the text regarding PDS, such as grain rot or diversion to the open market
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the policy shifts in India's food distribution strategy
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of PDS strengths and implementation failures (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Green Revolution: A Production Graph
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the statistical success of self-sufficiency in India
steps: Plot a line graph showing wheat and rice production from 1960 to the current decade
Explain the 'Wheat Revolution' term and the role of Indira Gandhi in promoting it
Analyze why certain states (Punjab/Haryana) gained more than others during this period
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Interprets agricultural data as a sign of national security
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the graph and historical context (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Nutrition and Child Welfare Schemes
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of non-grain food security interventions
steps: Research the 'Integrated Child Development Services' (ICDS) and its impact on the 'Infant Mortality Rate'
Evaluate the 'Mid-day Meal Scheme' as a tool for both nutrition and school attendance
Draft a short speech on why nutrition is as important as calorie intake
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the link between social welfare and national health
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and argumentative speech (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The MSP-Cropping Pattern Dilemma
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how government price support affects crop diversity
steps: Identify the crops primarily covered under MSP (Wheat and Rice)
Analyze how high MSP leads to 'Monoculture' and depletes groundwater in states like Punjab
Suggest why the government should provide MSP for pulses and coarse grains
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the environmental and economic trade-offs of MSP
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking on agricultural sustainability (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cooperatives and Market Stability
activities_projects.objective: Study alternative models of food security in the private/social sector
steps: Conduct a case study on 'Amul' or 'Mother Dairy' regarding price control for consumers
Analyze the 'Academy of Development Science' (ADS) grain bank model in Maharashtra
Explain how cooperatives reduce the influence of middlemen in the food chain
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the role of non-state actors in food distribution
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of the case study and cooperative analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Food Security Standards
activities_projects.objective: Compare India's food security status with the 1996 World Food Summit goals
steps: Define Food Security using the 1996 World Food Summit definition (Availability, Access, Utilization)
Explain why 'Self-sufficiency' in production doesn't always mean 'Food Security' for all households
Note the role of the 'National Food Security Act (2013)' in legalizing the right to food
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects national policies to international food security frameworks
activities_projects.evaluation: Conceptual clarity on the difference between production and access (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Designing a 'Zero Hunger' Community Plan
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize chapter concepts to solve local food insecurity
steps: Identify a community prone to seasonal hunger
Propose a 'Community Grain Bank' and a 'Fair Price Shop' improvement plan
Integrate the 'Mid-day Meal' and 'Aanganwadi' centers into your plan to ensure child nutrition
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies chapter concepts to practical rural development
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility and comprehensiveness of the plan (10 marks)
